title: SoCal Geoguesser
description: This project involved building a deep learning image-classification model for “SoCalGuessr,” a GeoGuessr-style game where the goal was to predict which Southern California city a Google Street View image came from. Using Python with PyTorch, Torchvision, NumPy, Pandas, and Scikit-learn, the project trained and evaluated neural network architectures on labeled image datasets while applying computer vision techniques such as image preprocessing and gradient-based optimization. The final system classified images across cities like Los Angeles, San Diego, and Riverside, with performance evaluated through accuracy metrics and confusion matrices.

title: LLM Audit Capstone
description: This project audits 11 widely used large language models to examine whether changing demographic cues in identical essays, resumes, legal cases, and patient reports leads to different outcomes across education, legal support, hiring, and mental-health contexts. Through interviews with instructors, hiring teams, legal aid organizations, clinicians, and product teams, the project also explores professional concerns about the safety and reliability of LLM deployment in high-impact domains. Rather than labeling models as fair or biased, the audit highlights where disparities emerge and emphasizes the importance of oversight and discussion before deploying these systems in real-world decision-making settings.

title: NBA Database and Modeling
description: This project built an end-to-end basketball analytics pipeline that scraped and processed NBA data from Basketball-Reference into a modeling-ready SQLite database for player projections, fantasy scoring analysis, and predictive modeling. Using Python, BeautifulSoup, Requests, Pandas, SQLite, and Scikit-learn, the system automated web scraping, data cleaning, ETL workflows, feature engineering, and machine learning experimentation while implementing rate-limiting and retry logic for reliable large-scale data collection. The project also included exploratory modeling workflows and statistical feature generation such as rolling averages, per-36 metrics, and player comparison analytics for fantasy basketball forecasting.

title: Bikewatching
description: An immersive, interactive map visualization of bike traffic in the Boston area during different times of the day.
